feed.title: Subject: Eltoo / LN-Symmetry on Inquisition Signet: BIP 118 + 3-Round State Chain (APO+CTV)

entry.title: Subject: Eltoo / LN-Symmetry on Inquisition Signet: BIP 118 + 3-Round State Chain (APO+CTV)
entry.summary:
In a recent development within the realm of Bitcoin transaction technologies, an alternative implementation of state chains incorporating both CSFS and CTV has been tested successfully. Unlike previous methods which required multiple transactions to achieve state updates, this new model achieves the Eltoo property with only two transactions. This efficiency is made possible through the use of Jeremy Rubin's CSFS rekey ladder construction, detailed in his blog post [here](https://rubin.io/bitcoin/2024/12/02/csfs-ctv-rekey-symmetry/). The construction allows for signature delegation via CSFS rekeys between states, rather than full transactions, significantly simplifying the entire chain into one settlement witness.
The technical execution of this model was enhanced by engineering refinements aimed at streamlining its implementation. These adjustments have proven effective as demonstrated on the Inquisition signet, where confirmed transaction IDs verify the operational success of this streamlined process. A comprehensive explanation of the full construction, along with a threat analysis and witness layout, can be accessed [here](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/eltoo-state-chain-on-signet-again-three-rounds-two-transactions-csfs-ctv-with-rekey-and-ladder-no-cat/2430), providing valuable insights into the practical application and security implications of this approach.
For those interested in exploring the source code of this implementation, it is available on GitHub. The repository, managed by developer Aaron, includes all necessary files and documentation to understand and experiment with this innovative approach. You can review the source code through the following [GitHub link](https://github.com/aaron-recompile/btcaaron/tree/main/examples/eltoo). This technological advancement represents a significant step forward in the efficiency and scalability of blockchain-based transactions, potentially influencing future developments in cryptocurrency protocols.
